chapters.title: The Hardware Story
chapters.description: Physical layer orientation. What an HCA is, why NICs became DPUs, how a DGX node is wired, the three separate networks.
chapters.previewSummary: Start here for the physical and architectural grounding behind every later chapter.

chapters.title: Operating Systems and Management Platforms
chapters.description: What runs on every device. How you access it after power-on. The management philosophy. CLI vs orchestrated. First power-on sequence.
chapters.previewSummary: Understand which software stack lives on which box before you touch the fabric.

chapters.title: Why HPC Networking Is Different
chapters.description: The AllReduce barrier, why TCP fails, tail latency math, and the mental model shift from enterprise to AI networking.
chapters.previewSummary: The conceptual shift from enterprise networking to synchronized GPU training workloads.

chapters.title: The CLI - Reading the Fabric
chapters.description: The commands and discipline for reading HPC fabric state. Which commands run where, how to read their output, and the investigation workflow from physical layer to configuration.
chapters.previewSummary: See how FabricLab teaches operators to read the fabric from DGX, switch, and UFM perspectives.

chapters.title: InfiniBand Operations - ONYX CLI and Fabric Management
chapters.description: The InfiniBand operations layer: ONYX CLI, error counter interpretation, Subnet Manager management, ibdiagnet fabric sweep, and UFM event correlation.
chapters.previewSummary: A deeper operational layer: ONYX, fabric sweeps, counters, and centralized management.

chapters.title: PFC, ECN, and Congestion Control
chapters.description: How losslessness actually works: PFC mechanics at the wire level, pause storm formation, ECN CE bit marking, DCQCN rate control algorithm, and the complete RoCEv2 port configuration checklist.
chapters.previewSummary: The chapter where AI Ethernet stops being marketing and becomes real queueing and congestion behavior.

chapters.title: Efficient Load Balancing
chapters.description: Why AI traffic is structurally low-entropy and how that breaks ECMP. The four load balancing modes (SLB, DLB, GLB, sDLB). Per-packet spraying and RSHP. In-cast congestion patterns and how to diagnose them from spine utilisation counters.
chapters.previewSummary: Why AI traffic defeats default ECMP and how modern fabrics restore path diversity.

chapters.title: Topology Design
chapters.description: How AI fabric scales from one switch to a SuperPOD. Fat-tree topology math, bisection bandwidth, BasePOD vs SuperPOD reference designs, oversubscription calculations, ROD vs RUD wiring, switch buffer selection, and cabling constraints.
chapters.previewSummary: Topology math, bisection bandwidth, and the design rules that separate workable fabrics from expensive mistakes.

chapters.title: NCCL - The Application Layer
chapters.description: How NCCL translates AllReduce into RDMA operations. Ring vs Tree vs Double-Binary Tree algorithms. The environment variables that determine whether NCCL finds RDMA or falls back to TCP. Reading nccl-tests output. Correlating busbw degradation to fabric diagnostics.
chapters.previewSummary: Bridge application behavior and fabric behavior by learning to read NCCL like an operator.

chapters.title: Optics, Cabling, and the Physical Layer
chapters.description: The physical layer beneath the fabric: 400G/800G optics, DSPs, fiber types, form factors, cable selection, and why signal integrity and power density now shape AI cluster design.
chapters.previewSummary: Learn the physical constraints and failure modes underneath the logical fabric.

chapters.title: The Storage Fabric
chapters.description: The separate network that feeds and protects training: storage isolation, GDS data paths, NVMe-oF transports, parallel file systems, checkpoint economics, and storage topology choices.
chapters.previewSummary: See how dataset ingress and checkpoint durability shape the second network in a training cluster.

chapters.title: Monitoring, Telemetry, and Observability
chapters.description: Know about problems before the ML engineer's Slack message arrives. UFM REST API, DCGM GPU metrics, Prometheus alert design, threshold calibration, and cross-layer correlation across four monitoring streams.
chapters.previewSummary: Monitoring and alert calibration across UFM, switches, GPUs, and fabric-wide symptoms.

chapters.title: Scale-Up Networking - NVLink Switch System
chapters.description: External NVLink Switch modules, 57.6 TB/s all-to-all at 256 GPUs, NVLink Network addressing, scale-up vs scale-out architecture decisions, and NVLink Switch diagnostics.
chapters.previewSummary: Move beyond scale-out and into the design tradeoffs of external NVLink switch fabrics.

chapters.title: Alternative Topologies
chapters.description: Torus, folded torus, dragonfly, and TPU Pod design choices - where they came from, what workloads they suit, and why fat-tree remains dominant for AI training clusters.
chapters.previewSummary: Compare non-fat-tree designs and learn why topology choice is always workload-driven.

chapters.title: GPU Hardware Generations
chapters.description: Network-relevant implications of GPU generations: NVLink/NVSwitch generation table, SXM vs PCIe form factors, GH200, H100 CNX, and Confidential Computing.
chapters.previewSummary: Track how GPU packaging and interconnect evolution changes the constraints on the network around it.

chapters.title: IP Routing for AI/ML Fabrics
chapters.description: How modern AI fabrics use routed Ethernet: BGP unnumbered, ASN design, BGP DPF, RIFT comparisons, Flex Algo, SRv6 path steering, and multi-tenant EVPN-VXLAN design.
chapters.previewSummary: Learn the routed-Ethernet patterns emerging underneath modern AI cluster fabrics.

chapters.title: The GPU Compute Network - Packet Anatomy
chapters.description: A packet-level walkthrough from NCCL work queue entries to remote DMA completion: DGX interfaces, Queue Pair mechanics, ConnectX-7 processing, switch forwarding, and end-to-end packet decode.
chapters.previewSummary: Walk a real training packet from work queue entry to remote completion and learn what each device actually does.

chapters.title: Storage Network Packet Path
chapters.description: A packet-level walkthrough of a checkpoint write from GPU HBM to storage appliance: NVMe-oF capsules, DMA paths, storage-fabric behavior, frame anatomy, and diagnostics.
chapters.previewSummary: Trace a storage checkpoint write byte by byte and see how the storage network differs from the compute fabric.

chapters.title: OOB and Management Network
chapters.description: The out-of-band management fabric: BMC architecture, IPMI and Redfish internals, OOB topology, switch management isolation, UFM communication paths, BlueField-3 management architecture, and hardening guidance.
chapters.previewSummary: Complete the three-fabric model by understanding the separate control-plane network that keeps clusters recoverable when the data paths fail.

chapters.title: IP Addressing and Planning
chapters.description: A complete addressing reference for DGX BasePOD and SuperPOD deployments: address families, RFC 1918 partitioning, loopback design, P2P links, /32 server routes, management-plane planning, VXLAN VNI allocation, and scaling pitfalls.
chapters.previewSummary: Plan every address family in the fabric before you rack hardware: loopbacks, links, servers, management, overlays, and growth.

chapters.title: Ultra Ethernet Consortium (UEC)
chapters.description: Why RoCEv2 has friction at scale, and how UEC addresses it: UET packet format, SACK-based reliability without lossless fabric, 1-RTT congestion feedback, native multipath spraying, switch requirements, and honest deployment readiness as of March 2026.
chapters.previewSummary: Understand where AI networking is going -- PFC elimination, end-to-end reliability, and native packet spraying -- and what it takes to actually deploy it.

chapters.title: Congestion Control Deep Dive
chapters.description: A rigorous, algorithm-level treatment of every congestion control scheme used in production AI fabrics — DCQCN, Swift, HPCC, TIMELY, and UEC CC — with practical guidance on parameter tuning and algorithm selection.
chapters.previewSummary: Understand how DCQCN, Swift, HPCC, TIMELY, and UEC CC differ — and which algorithm to choose for your fabric.

chapters.title: Segment Routing for AI Fabrics
chapters.description: A practitioner's guide to deploying SRv6, SR-TE, EVPN+SRv6, and IS-IS Flex-Algo in production AI data centre fabrics.
chapters.previewSummary: Learn how explicit path steering, SRv6 SIDs, EVPN integration, and Flex-Algo change traffic engineering in AI fabrics.

chapters.title: AI Networking Security
chapters.description: The complete security layer for AI fabrics: RDMA threat model, RoCEv2 RKEY protection, Spectrum-X GBP microsegmentation, InfiniBand PKey isolation, BlueField-3 as a security enforcement point, and UFM Cyber-AI anomaly detection.
chapters.previewSummary: See where AI fabric security actually lives: NIC GID filtering, RKEY entropy, Spectrum-X isolation, PKeys, and anomaly detection.

chapters.title: Spectrum-X Architecture and the AI Factory Platform
chapters.description: NVIDIA Spectrum-X: Spectrum-4 ASIC, BlueField-3 SuperNIC, DOCA, NetQ, and the vertically integrated Ethernet platform behind AI factory fabrics.
chapters.previewSummary: Map the Spectrum-X stack from ASIC and SuperNIC behavior to NVUE, NetQ, failure domains, and scalable unit design.

chapters.title: RoCE Configuration and Operations on Spectrum-X
chapters.description: Configure RoCEv2 end to end on Spectrum-X: prerequisites, NVUE Day-0 workflow, QoS architecture, ECN/PFC tuning, and production verification.
chapters.previewSummary: Turn the Spectrum-X hardware baseline into a functioning RoCE fabric with the exact QoS, ECN, and verification workflow operators use in production.

labs.title: Identify the failed rail
labs.description: A GPU rail has gone dark. Use the topology map and CLI tools to identify which rail, confirm the RDMA state, and isolate whether the fault is on the NIC or switch side.
labs.previewSummary: A first diagnostic lab that teaches the rail model and the basic DGX vs switch fault split.

labs.title: Fix the PFC misconfiguration
labs.description: A RoCEv2 workload is experiencing retransmissions. PFC is misconfigured. Diagnose and fix it.
labs.previewSummary: A guided lab on reading PFC state and repairing a broken RoCE configuration.

labs.title: Diagnose fabric congestion
labs.description: Throughput has dropped 40%. Investigate using interface counters and ECN configuration commands.
labs.previewSummary: Correlate switch-side symptoms with ECN and queue state to restore fabric health.

labs.title: Diagnose uneven spine utilisation
labs.description: AllReduce throughput has dropped with no drops visible. Diagnose why spine links are uneven and fix load balancing to restore full training throughput.
labs.previewSummary: A pathing lab focused on hot spines, unused links, and load-balancing diagnosis.

labs.title: Evaluate topology proposals
labs.description: Two vendors propose different switch configurations for a 64-node DGX H100 cluster. Calculate oversubscription ratios, identify which proposal meets requirements, and submit a recommendation before the purchase order is signed.
labs.previewSummary: Move from diagnosis to design by checking if a proposed fabric can actually support the cluster.

labs.title: Diagnose NCCL transport fallback
labs.description: A 16-node cluster shows 3 GB/s busbw instead of expected performance. All hardware is healthy. Diagnose why NCCL is using socket transport and fix the environment variable misconfiguration.
labs.previewSummary: Learn how application-level transport mistakes surface as fabric-performance symptoms.

labs.title: Triage a silent fabric degradation
labs.description: Training is 12% slower - but no hard errors anywhere. Use UFM port counters, DCGM GPU metrics, and switch counters to correlate a rising pre-FEC BER across three monitoring layers and identify the marginal physical connector before it becomes a full link failure.
labs.previewSummary: A cross-layer observability lab centered on correlation rather than obvious alarms.

labs.title: Uncover the hidden pause storm
labs.description: A switch looks healthy at a glance, but the NIC reveals a severe pause storm. Check both ends, identify missing ECN, and restore rate control before continuous PFC pauses collapse throughput.
labs.previewSummary: A lab about why one side of the link can look fine while the other is screaming.

labs.title: Fix the PFC priority mismatch
labs.description: PFC is enabled, but on the wrong traffic class. Cross-check NIC drops, PFC priority output, and RoCE DSCP-to-priority mapping, then move PFC protection back to priority 3.
labs.previewSummary: A lab about the deceptive case where PFC is technically on but protecting the wrong traffic.

labs.title: Recover the err-disabled rail
labs.description: A rail has gone err-disabled after a physical fault. Recognise the NIC-side active-state trap, confirm the switch port failure, replace the optic, clear err-disable, and verify full recovery.
labs.previewSummary: A physical-recovery lab that goes beyond diagnosis and into real return-to-service workflow.

labs.title: ECMP hotspot: BGP bandwidth community
labs.description: A reduced-capacity spine is still receiving equal ECMP traffic. Identify the missing BGP Link Bandwidth community and restore weighted ECMP before PFC storms spread.
labs.previewSummary: A routing-control lab for path weighting and hotspot prevention in AI fabrics.

labs.title: BGP suboptimal routing: spine ASN design
labs.description: A link failure triggers a bad 3-hop path because the spines use different ASNs. Trace the suboptimal route, unify the spine ASN design, and verify clean failover behavior.
labs.previewSummary: A lab about how routing policy choices show up as inefficient failover in production fabrics.

labs.title: SRv6 TE Path Steering
labs.description: Steer checkpoint-to-storage traffic onto a dedicated SRv6-TE path so collective traffic keeps the uncongested spine pool.
labs.previewSummary: Build and verify a DSCP-aware SRv6 path that moves checkpoint traffic off the shared NCCL spine pool.

labs.title: RDMA RKEY Exposure and Isolation
labs.description: Reproduce an RDMA RKEY exposure attack from a multi-tenant cluster, identify the two misconfigurations that enabled it, and apply GID filtering and RKEY rotation to harden the environment.
labs.previewSummary: Reproduce a cross-tenant RDMA exposure, then harden the cluster with GID filtering, RKEY rotation, and partition checks.

labs.title: Spectrum-X Platform Bring-Up Audit
labs.description: Audit a newly racked Spectrum-X scalable unit before RoCE configuration begins by verifying version, ports, ASIC identity, MTU baseline, and BGP cleanliness.
labs.previewSummary: Run the day-zero audit that catches platform mismatches before RoCE and NCCL troubleshooting starts.

labs.title: RoCE Day-Zero Configuration
labs.description: Apply the Day-0 Spectrum-X RoCE QoS profile, verify DSCP trust and PFC/ECN state, and validate the fabric with ib_write_bw and ib_write_lat.
labs.previewSummary: Run the RoCE bring-up workflow operators use before a new Spectrum-X training fabric is accepted.

labs.title: ECN Threshold Tuning Under Load
labs.description: Diagnose a broken ECN profile, compare thresholds to the carved lossless buffer, then restore DCQCN marking under multi-flow load.
labs.previewSummary: A tuning lab focused on the classic Spectrum-X defect where ECN thresholds sit above the physical lossless buffer budget.
